title: Couples Discount
weakest_section: 16:00-17:00 range with Clark's overly long fake stories

jokes.text: Or it's like me getting on my hands and knees and begging my partners to switch a Tuesday for a Thursday. Alakazam.
jokes.setup: Pete claims being together on Valentine's Day is 'like magic'
jokes.punchline: Reveals the pathetic reality behind the 'magic' with mock-magical language
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of romantic expectations with specific, relatable workplace humor and clever use of 'Alakazam'

jokes.text: And poof, he disappears.
jokes.setup: Continuing the magic metaphor
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about having to leave Erin again
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback that maintains the comedic thread while being self-deprecating

jokes.text: Bam, Andy! How do ya like me now?
jokes.setup: Erin declaring she's going to break up with Andy
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top aggressive gesture accompanying the declaration
jokes.explanation: Typical Erin physicality and awkward bravado, well-integrated with character but not particularly original

jokes.text: You know, when I was a kid we had a dog who got real sick, and we had to send him to a farm. And on his last day, we did everything he loved.
jokes.setup: Pete talking about doing whatever Erin wants on her last day with Andy
jokes.punchline: Comparing Erin to a dying dog being sent to a farm
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny metaphor that works on multiple levels - the 'farm' euphemism and the dying dog comparison is both sweet and morbid

jokes.text: Rink-a-dit doo, rika-doot-deet doo, rika-deet-doo.
jokes.setup: Dwight talking to imaginary Andy
jokes.punchline: Random nonsense sounds as Andy's response
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight weirdness - characteristic but random sounds don't have much comedic structure

jokes.text: I really like Andy these days. He's pretend, and he does exactly as I tell him to.
jokes.setup: Dwight enjoying fake Andy
jokes.punchline: Perfect manager is an imaginary one who obeys completely
jokes.explanation: Great character insight that's both funny and reveals Dwight's relationship with authority perfectly

jokes.text: Unless he comes back as pretend Dwight. In which case, we're in for an epic, confusing showdown.
jokes.setup: Dwight's fear about real Andy returning
jokes.punchline: Imagining Andy pretending to be Dwight in response
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that creates a hilarious mental image of competing impersonations

jokes.text: So, I guess it's good-bye, chunky lemon milk.
jokes.setup: Kevin has been drinking Andy's milk for three months
jokes.punchline: The milk is now 'chunky lemon' - spoiled beyond recognition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - disgusting but said with genuine affection. 'Chunky lemon milk' is hilariously specific

jokes.text: Yeah, let's get some booze and some cocaine and just blow it out, no consequences.
jokes.setup: Phyllis suggests having a fun boss-less day
jokes.punchline: Clark immediately jumps to drugs and alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Clark's character while contrasting with the innocent suggestion

jokes.text: Or the mini-mall.
jokes.setup: Clark's extreme suggestion
jokes.punchline: Phyllis completely ignores it and suggests the mini-mall instead
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan Phyllis response that completely deflates Clark's intensity with mundane suggestion

jokes.text: But I mean, if you don't have one of those, you can probably just bring whoever it is you use to kill your loneliness.
jokes.setup: Explaining couples discount at mini-mall
jokes.punchline: Darkly accurate description of what fake relationships are for
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark and accurate observation from Phyllis delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Phyll, I'll pretend to be your husband. I'm already sick of you, so it'll seem realistic.
jokes.setup: Stanley offering to be Phyllis's fake husband
jokes.punchline: His reasoning is that he already dislikes her enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line - brutally honest, perfectly structured, and completely in character

jokes.text: They use a watch repair kit.
jokes.setup: Phyllis talking about getting her feet detailed
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of using watch repair tools for pedicures
jokes.explanation: Weirdly specific detail that creates a vivid, absurd mental image

jokes.text: No. Yes. Yes. Why wouldn't I?
jokes.setup: Oscar asking Darryl to pretend to be a couple
jokes.punchline: Darryl's quick reversal from rejection to acceptance
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing Darryl's thought process in real time

jokes.text: I wanna pretend to be gay. I got no problem with that.
jokes.setup: Darryl agreeing to fake relationship with Oscar
jokes.punchline: His overly emphatic declaration about being fine with it
jokes.explanation: Typical awkward overcompensation that creates mild cringe humor

jokes.text: Stop talking about it. I said I'm fine with it.
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to give advice about their fake relationship
jokes.punchline: Darryl's defensive insistence that he's comfortable
jokes.explanation: Continues the awkward overcompensation theme effectively

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's less romantic.
jokes.setup: Jim mentioning their romantic lunch is with Brian and Alyssa
jokes.punchline: Pam's immediate deflation at the revelation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation joke with perfect economy

jokes.text: Your boyfriend. He look like a pretty girl.
jokes.setup: Angela and Clark pretending to be a couple
jokes.punchline: Worker commenting on Clark's feminine appearance
jokes.explanation: Basic appearance-based humor that works for the situation but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: And you know what? I think you are developing. We should go and get you a training bra.
jokes.setup: Playing along with Clark looking like a girl
jokes.punchline: Angela taking it further by treating Clark like a developing teen
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Angela's mean streak while building on the setup

jokes.text: She's living a lie.
jokes.setup: Clark upset about losing the discount
jokes.punchline: Accusing Angela of 'living a lie' about their fake relationship
jokes.explanation: Ironic complaint about dishonesty in their mutually agreed upon deception

jokes.text: Turns out I can't even be in a pretend relationship.
jokes.setup: Clark's failed fake relationship with Angela
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his relationship failures extending to fake ones
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware line that turns relationship incompetence into a broader character trait

jokes.text: Oh, it works. Him and me, all right, we are crazy in love. More in love than your small mind can comprehend.
jokes.setup: Being denied couples discount for being two men
jokes.punchline: Darryl's over-the-top passionate declaration of fake love
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Darryl's commitment to the bit while being righteously indignant

jokes.text: And we have two disposable incomes and no kids, and we're taking our business elsewhere.
jokes.setup: Continuing his indignant speech about their fake relationship
jokes.punchline: Making an economic argument while staying in character
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that makes a real economic point while maintaining the fake relationship premise

jokes.text: We're splitting up.
jokes.setup: Jim asking where Alyssa is
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of marriage ending during what should be a thank-you lunch
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment through casual delivery of devastating personal news

jokes.text: You're back. And you're disgusting.
jokes.setup: Real Andy returning to interrupt fake Andy scenario
jokes.punchline: Dwight's blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight response - no filter, completely honest, economically delivered

jokes.text: I guess I can cancel my order from zappos.com because, oh, the loafers have arrived.
jokes.setup: Andy returning from his boat trip
jokes.punchline: Calling Andy a loafer (both lazy person and type of shoe)
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning delivered with Angela's characteristic passive-aggressive precision

jokes.text: I am really, really bad at breakups. Technically, I'm still dating my first-grade boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Erin struggling to break up with Andy
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's still technically dating someone from first grade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin logic that escalates from current problem to absurd past behavior

jokes.text: I mean, we just had our 20th anniversary. And I forgot to get him something.
jokes.setup: Still dating first-grade boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Feeling guilty about forgetting their anniversary gift
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats the absurd premise with complete sincerity, perfectly Erin

jokes.text: It's called bembe. ♪ Yoruba yoruba aruba yoruba yoruba yoruba aruba raruba ♪
jokes.setup: Andy showing off his island instruments and culture
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical fake African/Caribbean singing
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy cultural appropriation cringe but the execution is more annoying than funny

jokes.text: Hey, burning man, if it's not selling out too much, you might wanna throw on a tie.
jokes.setup: Andy's hippie appearance and behavior
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calling wearing a tie 'selling out' while pointing out professional necessity
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic dig that highlights Andy's disconnect from workplace reality

jokes.text: It's in every bembe cafe.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to stay connected via internet in Caribbean
jokes.punchline: Calling cafes 'bembe cafes' using his made-up musical term
jokes.explanation: Shows Andy's tendency to incorporate his new obsessions into everything, mildly funny callback

jokes.text: At least my crying won't get you fired.
jokes.setup: Pam and Brian discussing their need to stop breaking down in front of each other
jokes.punchline: Dark reference to Brian losing his job for protecting her
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that acknowledges the real consequences of their emotional connection

jokes.text: Just say the word 'coolio.'
jokes.setup: Andy wanting Dwight to acknowledge his authority
jokes.punchline: Demanding Dwight say his ridiculous made-up word
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe Andy behavior but the word choice is dated and not particularly clever

jokes.text: that was not... how I had hoped that would go.
jokes.setup: Andy's call to Jan resulting in losing the biggest sale
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about the disaster he just caused
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement reaction to complete catastrophe, well-executed but familiar

jokes.text: Two seconds of the turd dog, and he loses the biggest sale this branch has ever seen.
jokes.setup: Dwight angry about Andy losing the Jan sale
jokes.punchline: Playing on Andy's 'Nard Dog' nickname by calling him 'turd dog'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective name play that captures Dwight's disgust

jokes.text: All out. I mean like everything was out the whole week.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing how hard he worked to get the Jan sale
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably suggestive description of his sales efforts
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight overshare with unintentional sexual undertones

jokes.text: He just waltzes back in here like he owns the chunky lemon milk.
jokes.setup: Dwight complaining about Andy's entitled return
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kevin's spoiled milk situation as metaphor for ownership
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that uses the earlier milk joke as metaphor for Andy's entitlement

jokes.text: I'd like to rat out Andy, but unfortunately I have a bit of a 'boy who cried wolf' dynamic with David Wallace. Except instead of a boy I'm a man, and instead of a wolf, I cried, 'genetically-engineered monster wolf.'
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he can't tell Wallace about Andy
jokes.punchline: Overly literal correction that makes the metaphor more absurd
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic that escalates a simple metaphor into something completely ridiculous and character-specific

jokes.text: Meredith Palmer ain't never been called no narc. Floozy? Yes. Alkie? Check. Einstein sarcastically? You bet. But never no narc.
jokes.setup: Being asked to rat out Andy
jokes.punchline: Listing all the things she's been called except narc
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith self-awareness with great escalating list of insults she embraces

jokes.text: Vomit mop? Sure. Floor meat? That's me. Flesh hoover? Hi.
jokes.setup: Continuing her list of accepted nicknames
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting and specific terms she's fine with
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting escalation with increasingly creative and specific insults. 'Flesh hoover' is particularly memorable

jokes.text: ♪ Who's that girl? ♪ It's Andy.
jokes.setup: Andy singing the 'New Girl' theme song
jokes.punchline: Oscar's deadpan identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar deadpan response to Andy's ridiculous entrance

jokes.text: Lit the whole thing on fire. It was crazy. The whole thing is in ashes. Fire department was here. It was in all the papers.
jokes.setup: Clark making up stories for Andy about what he missed
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake story about Pam's mural being destroyed
jokes.explanation: Good escalating fake story but goes on a bit long without additional punchlines

jokes.text: And, uh, Kathy Ireland signed on as the official spokesbabe of Dunder Mifflin.
jokes.setup: Continuing to make up outrageous stories
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous celebrity endorsement story
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation but the choice of Kathy Ireland feels random rather than cleverly specific

jokes.text: Yeah, in the European billboards, she's gonna be topless.
jokes.setup: The Kathy Ireland endorsement story
jokes.punchline: Adding inappropriate topless element
jokes.explanation: Cheap escalation relying on sexual reference without much cleverness

jokes.text: I am speaking metaphorically, of course. You know I have lots of irons in the 'fire.' Well, that's one of 'em, you know. Making sure that the warehouse logistics is a well-oiled, properly-stoked fire.
jokes.setup: Andy caught in his lie about warehouse fire
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward attempt to cover with mixed metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy panic response with completely garbled metaphors creating strong cringe

jokes.text: Really playing the scales, huh?
jokes.setup: Erin playing with the fish toy
jokes.punchline: Fish scales/musical scales pun
jokes.explanation: Basic pun that works in context but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: I mean, my parents lasted 30, 40 years. They were never happy at the same time.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to convince Erin they have a future
jokes.punchline: Describing his parents' loveless marriage as a positive example
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny insight that perfectly captures dysfunctional relationship logic with great economy

jokes.text: I know you may not be feeling love for me right now, but if you fake it, I won't be able to tell the difference. So I'll feel good.
jokes.setup: Andy's desperate attempt to save the relationship
jokes.punchline: Asking Erin to fake love because he can't tell the difference
jokes.explanation: Painfully honest self-awareness that's both pathetic and funny, very Andy

jokes.text: All right, put your dukes up, Beesly.
jokes.setup: Pam saying they should fight instead of avoiding conflict
jokes.punchline: Jim's playful boxing stance in response to emotional fight suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim move to diffuse tension with playful misinterpretation, very character-appropriate

jokes.text: What was that about three months?
jokes.setup: The entire episode building to Wallace not knowing Andy was gone
jokes.punchline: Wallace overhearing and questioning the three months comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending payoff that resolves the main tension with elegant simplicity

tmdb_overview: Staff pair up for Valentine's mall discount; Erin plans to break up with Andy.
